edges.description: An extra X chromosome elevates the dosage of X-linked escape genes, perturbing chromatin and transcription beyond what is observed in 46,XY.
edges.target: X-chromosome Inactivation Escape / Gene Dosage Imbalance

edges.description: 47,XXY karyotype drives progressive seminiferous-tubule, Sertoli/Leydig, and microvascular dysfunction in the testis.
X-chromosome dosage imbalance contributes to developmental delay and language vulnerability in 47,XXY.
intermediate_mechanisms: sex-chromosome dosage effects on neurodevelopmental language networks
edges.source: X-chromosome Inactivation Escape / Gene Dosage Imbalance

edges.description: X-linked dosage imbalance contributes to the language-based learning disability risk in Klinefelter syndrome.
intermediate_mechanisms: sex-chromosome dosage effects on cognitive and language development
edges.source: X-chromosome Inactivation Escape / Gene Dosage Imbalance

edges.description: X-chromosome dosage imbalance contributes to executive dysfunction and ADHD-like attention problems.
intermediate_mechanisms: sex-chromosome dosage effects on executive function
edges.source: X-chromosome Inactivation Escape / Gene Dosage Imbalance

edges.description: Extra sex-chromosome dosage, including pseudoautosomal-region effects, contributes to tall stature in Klinefelter syndrome.
edges.source: X-chromosome Inactivation Escape / Gene Dosage Imbalance

edges.description: Sertoli-cell and seminiferous tubule dysfunction drives germ-cell apoptosis and progressive depletion through puberty.
Leydig-cell dysfunction reduces testosterone production while elevated LH/FSH reflect loss of negative feedback, producing primary hypogonadism.
Seminiferous-tubule degeneration, Sertoli/Leydig dysfunction, and germ-cell loss produce small testes.
Progressive germ-cell loss and spermatogenic failure produce azoospermia.
Hypogonadism and altered sex-steroid balance predispose to gynecomastia.
intermediate_mechanisms: androgen deficiency and altered estrogen-androgen balance

edges.description: Hypogonadism in Klinefelter syndrome is associated with impaired metabolic profile, including insulin resistance.
Hypogonadism contributes to reduced bone density, including osteopenia.
intermediate_mechanisms: androgen deficiency effects on bone mineralization

edges.description: Metabolic disturbances and thrombotic tendency in Klinefelter syndrome increase cardiovascular disease risk.

evidence.reference_title: ADHD and genetic syndromes.
evidence.snippet: A high rate of Attention Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD)-like characteristics has been reported in a wide variety of disorders including syndromes with known genetic causes... and Klinefelter Syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Effect of sex chromosome number variation on attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder symptoms, executive function, and processing speed.
evidence.snippet: X chromosome excess was associated with weakness in working memory (p=0.018) and approached significance for attention problems (p=0.071)...

evidence.reference_title: Klinefelter syndrome: more than hypogonadism.
evidence.snippet: Azoospermia and infertility are almost inevitably present

evidence.reference_title: Klinefelter syndrome: going beyond the diagnosis.
evidence.snippet: Although Klinefelter syndrome (KS) is common, it is rarely recognised in childhood, sometimes being identified with speech or developmental delay or incidental antenatal diagnosis... Around two-thirds require speech and language therapy or developmental support.

evidence.reference_title: The spectrum of the behavioral phenotype in boys and adolescents 47,XXY (Klinefelter syndrome).
evidence.snippet: The behavioral phenotype of 47,XXY (Klinefelter syndrome) includes increased risks for developmental delays, language-based learning disabilities...

evidence.reference_title: Klinefelter syndrome: more than hypogonadism.
evidence.snippet: The classical phenotype is that of a tall male with relatively long legs, small, firm testes and gynecomastia.

nodes.description: Leydig cell insufficiency and impaired testosterone export result in elevated LH/FSH and low systemic testosterone despite potentially elevated intratesticular testosterone.

evidence.reference_title: Klinefelter syndrome: more than hypogonadism.
evidence.snippet: Hypogonadism is usually not evident until early adulthood and progresses with ageing.

evidence.reference_title: Anatomical and clinical aspects of Klinefelter's syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Klinefelter's syndrome, the most common sex disorder associated with chromosomal aberrations, is characterized by a plethora of clinical features.

evidence.reference_title: Hypogonadism: Is It Always Hypogonadotropic in an Adolescent With a Cleft Palate? A Surprising Case of Klinefelter Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: presented with hypoglycemia due to isolated secondary adrenal insufficiency, who further had a decrease in testicular size with increased follicle-stimulating hormone level (hypergonadotropic hypogonadism) and diagnosed with Klinefelter syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Klinefelter syndrome: more than hypogonadism.
evidence.snippet: a tendency to thrombosis, which all favor cardiovascular disease.

evidence.reference_title: Klinefelter syndrome: more than hypogonadism.
evidence.snippet: impaired metabolic profile (obesity, dyslipidemia, insulin resistance)

evidence.reference_title: The cognitive phenotype in Klinefelter syndrome: a review of the literature including genetic and hormonal factors.
evidence.snippet: Most studies support that males with KS have an increased risk of language disorders and reading disabilities.

evidence.reference_title: The spectrum of the behavioral phenotype in boys and adolescents 47,XXY (Klinefelter syndrome).
evidence.snippet: The behavioral phenotype of 47,XXY (Klinefelter syndrome) includes increased risks for developmental delays, language-based learning disabilities, executive dysfunction/ADHD, and socialemotional difficulties.

evidence.reference_title: Endocrine aspects of Klinefelter syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Truncal adiposity, metabolic syndrome and low bone mass occur frequently.

nodes.description: Typically results in a 47,XXY karyotype, leading to a range of physical, developmental, and reproductive issues.

evidence.reference_title: Klinefelter syndrome and other sex chromosomal aneuploidies.
evidence.snippet: The term Klinefelter syndrome (KS) describes a group of chromosomal disorder in which there is at least one extra X chromosome to a normal male karyotype, 46,XY. XXY aneuploidy is the most common disorder of sex chromosomes in humans, with prevalence of one in 500 males.

evidence.reference_title: Epigenetics and genomics in Klinefelter syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Since the first description of Klinefelter syndrome (KS) was published in 1942...large inter-individual variability in the phenotypic presentation has been demonstrated... Evidence from the existing literature of KS indicates that not just one single genetic mechanism can explain the phenotype and the variable expressivity.

evidence.reference_title: Neuropsychology and socioeconomic aspects of Klinefelter syndrome: new developments.
evidence.snippet: Klinefelter syndrome is the most common sex-chromosome disorder in humans, affecting one in 660 men. The key findings in Klinefelter syndrome are small testes, hypergonadotropic hypogonadism and cognitive impairment.

nodes.description: Accelerated germ cell apoptosis beginning around puberty leads to spermatogenic failure and azoospermia in adulthood.

evidence.reference_title: Neuropsychology and socioeconomic aspects of Klinefelter syndrome: new developments.
evidence.snippet: The key findings in Klinefelter syndrome are small testes, hypergonadotropic hypogonadism and cognitive impairment.

evidence.reference_title: Chromosomal variants in klinefelter syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The typical symptoms are a tall stature...

nodes.description: Immature Sertoli and Leydig cells, proinflammatory macrophage enrichment, extracellular matrix remodeling and microvascular dysfunction impair spermatogenesis and testosterone export.

In 47,XXY, the inactive X chromosome (Xi) does not fully silence expression of a subset of X-linked escape genes (notably DDX3X, USP9X, KDM6A, and other dosage-sensitive loci). The resulting bi-allelic expression of these genes elevates effective gene dosage above the typical male single-X level, contributing to neurodevelopmental, cognitive, and metabolic phenotypes independent of androgen deficiency. The Xi also exerts trans regulatory effects on active-X and autosomal gene expression, broadening the molecular impact of sex-chromosome aneuploidy beyond classic escape.
nodes.id: X-chromosome Inactivation Escape / Gene Dosage Imbalance

evidence.reference_title: X chromosome dosage and the genetic impact across human tissues
evidence.snippet: Fourteen X chromosomal genes were downregulated in 45,X and upregulated in 47,XXY, respectively, in all three tissues (AKAP17A,CD99,DHRSX,EIF2S3,GTPBP6,JPX,KDM6A,PP2R3B,PUDP,SLC25A6,TSIX,XIST,ZBED1,ZFX).

evidence.snippet: Evidence from the existing literature of KS indicates that not just one single genetic mechanism can explain the phenotype and the variable expressivity.
